atomicity.message: Staged payload implements only dogfood mode for orchestrator findings-log instructions, its config/default plumbing, scaffold/help/robot tests, and the tracker bead.

conditions.message: Staged payload passes formatting and relevant Go test suites.
evidence: git diff --cached --check exited 0.
go test ./internal/config ./internal/scaffold passed.
go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run Init|Config|Robots|Help|Dogfood|Orchestrator passed.
go test ./... passed across all packages.

conditions.message: Implementation reuses existing init/config/scaffold paths instead of creating a parallel dogfood subsystem.
evidence: defaults.init.dogfood is added to existing InitDefaults merge/source tracking, configSettingValue, robot stdin, and runInit plumbing.
ORCHESTRATOR rendering uses the new renderOrchestratorTemplate path following the existing template pattern.
Tests extend existing config/init/scaffold suites with focused dogfood cases.

conditions.message: Feature encourages logging friction without turning findings into self-approval or gate shortcuts.
evidence: Staged ORCHESTRATOR templates render the dogfood block only when Dogfood is true, so default output is unchanged.
Template text tells orchestrators to keep findings factual and explicitly says findings do not approve scope changes, publishing, history rewrite, or gate bypass.
--no-dogfood and explicit --dogfood/--no-dogfood conflict rejection prevent ambiguous override behavior.
Generated-output and robot dogfood=false smokes confirmed opt-in/on and explicit off behavior.

conditions.message: New behavior has direct tests for CLI flag, config default, robot input, disabled override, template conditional behavior, help text, and source tracking.
evidence: Tests include TestInitDogfoodFlagAddsOrchestratorFindingsBlock, TestInitNoDogfoodOverridesConfigDefault, robot dogfood input coverage, TestApplyInitCanCreateDogfoodOrchestratorTarget, and TestOrchestratorTemplateDogfoodBlockIsConditional.
Config/help tests cover defaults.init.dogfood source tracking and robot/help documentation.
Focused tests passed for internal/config, internal/scaffold, and cmd/burpvalve init/config/robots/help/orchestrator paths.

conditions.message: The staged slice satisfies the light dogfood-mode acceptance criteria across CLI, robot/config, template rendering, help, and tests.
evidence: hash --staged reported sha256:7f3b608baa0f73f7e1b4adff3999cebee4e2b30d8146bcd92fc70f1e0d45808c and the 13 packet paths with no excluded staged payload.
Staged code wires defaults.init.dogfood, --dogfood/--no-dogfood, robot stdin support, config source reporting, and conditional ORCHESTRATOR.md rendering.
Staged tests cover direct --dogfood, config default, --no-dogfood override, robot dogfood input, help/robot docs, config/source merge, and default-no-block behavior.
go test ./internal/config ./internal/scaffold and go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run Init|Config|Robots|Help|Dogfood|Orchestrator passed.
Smoke init --force --json orchestrator --dogfood created ORCHESTRATOR.md with Dogfood Findings, docs/dogfooding-findings-YYYY-MM.md, Why it matters, and How-to-apply.

conditions.message: The staged tracker entry and tests document the feature intent and evidence surface.
evidence: Staged .beads entry for project-readiness-kit-dogfood-mode-orchestrator-findings-qkv is in_progress with labels config,dogfood,orchestrator,scaffold,v0-1-3-blocker and records owner spec, scope, non-goals, and acceptance criteria.
Smoke init --dogfood created ORCHESTRATOR.md containing Dogfood Findings, docs/dogfooding-findings-YYYY-MM.md, Why it matters, and How-to-apply.
Robot help checks show --robots init -h and --robots config init -h document dogfood/findings-log fields.

conditions.message: Governed staged payload stays inside dogfood-mode scope: opt-in config/CLI/template/help/tests plus tracker state.
evidence: git diff --cached --name-status listed only .beads/issues.jsonl plus cmd/burpvalve config/help/init/main, internal/config, internal/scaffold, and ORCHESTRATOR.md template paths.
hash --staged included exactly those 13 staged paths and no generated evidence files.
Unrelated README/docs/plans dirt was unstaged and absent from staged hash/path output.
Staged hunks add no release cut, visibility flip, publishing, history rewrite, NTM automation, Agent Mail sending, or automatic findings capture.

conditions.message: Implementation adds config/template/help/test behavior only and no destructive operations or release/visibility actions.
evidence: Staged diff search for destructive/security terms found no new destructive command path.
Generated Dogfood Findings block explicitly says not to treat a finding as approval to change scope, publish, rewrite history, or bypass the gate.
Staged path list contains code/config/scaffold/template/test files plus .beads only; no release, visibility, install, workflow, or deletion artifact is staged.

conditions.message: Staged hash/path list matches the packet, and the new config field merges and reports sources correctly.
evidence: hash --staged returned sha256:7f3b608baa0f73f7e1b4adff3999cebee4e2b30d8146bcd92fc70f1e0d45808c with the 13 packet paths and no excluded staged payload.
git diff --cached --name-status matched the packet path list exactly.
internal/config/config.go merges Dogfood via overrideBool and source-tracks via noteBool.
Config tests passed and include defaults.init.dogfood project-source assertions.

conditions.message: Dogfood findings remain local/manual with no new secret-handling, external communication, or privileged execution boundary.
evidence: Staged code adds boolean config/flag plumbing and scaffold template rendering only; no network, credential, shell execution, Agent Mail, NTM, or filesystem monitoring behavior.
ORCHESTRATOR template instructs local findings-log documentation under docs/dogfooding-findings-YYYY-MM.md and does not request secrets, token capture, external submission, or telemetry.
git diff --cached --check passed.

conditions.message: No governed visual rendering surface is staged.
evidence: Staged payload changes CLI/config/scaffold/template/test files and .beads only; it does not change Bubble Tea views, Lip Gloss styling, screenshots, SVGs, demos, CSS, browser UI, or visual assets.
ORCHESTRATOR.md output was smoke-checked as text content, not a visual surface.

conditions.message: No performance-sensitive runtime path or benchmarked behavior is staged.
evidence: Runtime work is a boolean option read and conditional template block during init only.
No loops over large data, concurrency, I/O-heavy processing, or persistent runtime hot path is added.

conditions.message: Mode creates an operator prompt, not an autonomous workflow engine; owner and gate authority remain intact.
evidence: Read-only reference /home/ubuntu/Projects/outbound-agent/CLAUDE.md contains terse orchestrator conduct and dogfood-findings logging; staged block follows that style for ORCHESTRATOR.md.
Template asks the orchestrator to log issues/complications and follow-ups while explicitly denying that findings authorize scope changes, publishing, history rewrite, or gate bypass.
Staged code only passes Dogfood into ApplyInitWithOptions and renderOrchestratorTemplate; there is no automatic findings entry creation or work execution based on findings.
